templates.description:
What it does: Prioritizes staff rest by strictly limiting weekly hours.
How it works: Applies a soft cap on the maximum number of blocks a staff member can work per week, overriding standard limits to enforce lighter, healthier workloads.
Why it's useful: Prevents burnout during slow periods and promotes a better work-life balance for the team.

What it does: Encourages maximum capacity and overtime shifts to handle surge demand.
How it works: Provides a mathematical reward (negative penalty) to the engine every time it successfully schedules a 12-hour (3 block) overtime shift, aggressively pulling the system toward maximum resource utilization.
Why it's useful: Essential during holiday rushes or major promotional events when you need every available hour from your staff.

What it does: Allows 'Close-then-Open' shifts during severe staff shortages to prevent branch closure.
How it works: Converts strict rest laws into soft guidelines. The engine will schedule a back-to-back late night/early morning shift if it's the absolutely only way to keep the branch operational, but applies a massive penalty to discourage casual use.
Why it's useful: Acts as an emergency safety valve. It prevents the math engine from crashing when a sudden sick leave makes a legal roster impossible.

What it does: Improves staff safety by ensuring no one works the late night shift completely alone.
How it works: Specifically targets the 20:00 block and enforces a minimum headcount of 2. If the engine is forced to schedule only one person due to constraints, it applies a heavy safety penalty.
Why it's useful: Crucial for security and operational compliance during high-risk graveyard hours.

What it does: Prevents staff burnout by guaranteeing at least one weekend day off.
How it works: Scans only Saturdays and Sundays. If the engine schedules the same employee to work on both days, it triggers a significant fairness penalty, forcing the engine to distribute weekend loads evenly.
Why it's useful: Keeps morale high by ensuring that weekend work is equitably shared across the entire team.

What it does: Strictly limits the maximum weekly hours for a specific team to comply with part-time contracts.
How it works: Uses an Individual Aggregator on a Weekly timeframe to restrict total working blocks.
Why it's useful: You can apply this directly to a 'Junior' or 'Student' team to ensure you never accidentally overschedule them beyond their legal or contractual limits.

What it does: Ensures that every staff member is assigned a minimum baseline of hours per week.
How it works: Uses an Individual Aggregator to mandate a minimum number of weekly blocks, applying a heavy penalty if the engine 'starves' an employee of shifts.
Why it's useful: Protects staff livelihoods during slow periods, reducing turnover by guaranteeing a baseline income.

What it does: Prevents overstaffing during the lowest-revenue night shifts.
How it works: Uses a Collective Aggregator specifically targeting the 00:00 block, capping the total number of staff allowed to work during that time.
Why it's useful: Even if the engine wants to assign more people to balance out weekly hours, this rule acts as a strict ceiling to protect your profit margins during quiet hours.

What it does: Prevents short, intense bursts of overwork (e.g., three 12-hour days in a row).
How it works: Slides a 3-day mathematical window across the schedule and strictly caps the total number of blocks an individual can work within that brief period.
Why it's useful: While 'Wellness Mode' looks at the whole week, this rule prevents the dangerous short-term sprints that are a leading cause of immediate burnout and operational mistakes.

What it does: An experimental scheduling mode that condenses the work week.
How it works: Uses the 'working_days' timeframe to force the engine to give everyone at least 3 full days off per week, often relying on 8h or 12h shifts on their working days to meet demand.
Why it's useful: A highly attractive modern perk for staff retention, proving that the business can operate efficiently while giving employees massive blocks of uninterrupted time off.
